DECLARATION OF SAMUEL SAZER IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE APPLICATION TO VACATE TRIAL DATE AND PRE-TRIAL DEADLINES (TRANSACTION ID # 77089428) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BHSD 505 PROPCO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY BHSD TPC PROPCO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS BHSD 505 PROPOCO LLC AND BHSD TPC PROPCO LLC'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE APPEARING, THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO DEPOSITION OF MICHAEL SHVO SHALL OCCUR UNDER ANY NOTICE OR SUBPOENA NOW EXTANT IN THIS ACTION. (SEE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2025.420(B).)AS ALTERNATIVE GROUNDS FOR GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION, THE COURT FINDS PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO OPPOSE THE MOTION AMOUNTS TO CONSENT TO THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. (SEE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 8.54(C).)COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs BHSD 505 PROPCO LLC, BHSD TPC PROPCO LLC.'s Motion For Protective Hearing.

Plaintiffs BHSD 505 PROPOCO LLC and BHSD TPC PROPCO LLC's unopposed Motion for Protective Order is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the court orders that no deposition of Michael Shvo shall occur under any notice or subpoena now extant in this action. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.420(b).)

As alternative grounds for granting Defendant's motion, the court finds Plaintiff's failure to oppose the motion amounts to consent to the granting of the motion. (See California Rules of Court, rule 8.54(c).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Excess Pages Of Plaintiffs' Reply Brief In Support Of Its Motion For Summary Adjudication For Exceeding The Page Limit
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samuel Sazer and Matthew Covinton appeared for plaintiffs BHSD 505 Propco LLC and BHSD TPC Propco LLC.
Spencer Hosie appeared for Defendant Hosie Rice, LLP.

Not contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE EXCESS PAGES OF PLAINTIFFS' REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR EXCEEDING THE PAGE LIMIT is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. The Court notes that the Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of the Reply, excluding the captions page, Table of Contents and Table of Authorities, is 10 pages long.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samuel Sazer and Matthew Covinton appeared for plaintiffs BHSD 505 Propco LLC and BHSD TPC Propco LLC.
Spencer Hosie appeared for Defendant Hosie Rice, LLP.

Plaintiffs contested the Court's tentative decision to deny plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication.

Mr. Sazer argued that his notice complied with CRC 3.1350(b) in that it stated the cause of action verbatim, and that the entire sentence need not match the separate statement in its entirety.

Mr. Hosie countered that Mr. Sazer misinterprets CRC 3.1350(b) and argued in support of the Court's tentative decision.

Mr. Sazer requested that the Court exercise its discretion and waive procedural defects, citing concerns regarding their currently set trial in October and their inability to refile.

The Court opted to grant Mr. Sazer's request to exercise its discretion and ruled as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order with a statement re: compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) and in accordance with CCP Sec. 437c(g). Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581, angiediner@gmail.com, 415-748-4030. (D501)
